evals.prompt: I'm building a product catalog page in Next.js App Router. It needs: a server component page that fetches products, a client component for search/filter with debounced input, a product card grid that handles loading/empty/error states, and a 'Add to Cart' button with optimistic updates. Use TanStack Query for data fetching and Zustand for cart state.
evals.expected_output: Server component page, client search component with useDebouncedValue, TanStack Query hooks, Zustand cart store, error boundary, loading skeletons, empty state

assertions.name: server_component_default
assertions.description: Page component is a server component (no 'use client' directive) that fetches data directly

assertions.name: client_pushed_down
assertions.description: 'use client' only on interactive components (search, button), not on the page itself

assertions.name: tanstack_query_used
assertions.description: Uses useQuery/useMutation from @tanstack/react-query for data fetching, not raw useEffect

assertions.name: zustand_store
assertions.description: Cart state managed with Zustand create() store, not useState or Context

assertions.name: loading_error_empty_states
assertions.description: All three states handled: loading skeleton, error message, empty state with CTA

assertions.name: debounced_search
assertions.description: Search input uses debouncing (custom hook or setTimeout pattern)

evals.prompt: Create wireframes for an admin dashboard with these screens: 1) Dashboard overview with KPI cards and recent activity feed, 2) User management table with search, filters, and bulk actions, 3) Settings page with tabs for profile, notifications, and billing. Include navigation flow, responsive breakpoints, and component states.
evals.expected_output: ASCII wireframes for all 3 screens, component tables with data sources, all 4 states per screen, responsive breakpoints, navigation flow Mermaid diagram, interaction specs

assertions.name: ascii_wireframes
assertions.description: At least 3 ASCII art wireframe layouts using box-drawing characters

assertions.name: component_tables
assertions.description: Component tables with columns for name, data source, and actions

assertions.name: four_states
assertions.description: Loading, empty, error, and loaded states specified for each screen

assertions.name: responsive_breakpoints
assertions.description: Mobile, tablet, desktop breakpoints defined with layout changes

assertions.name: navigation_flow
assertions.description: Mermaid flowchart showing navigation between screens

assertions.name: interaction_specs
assertions.description: At least one interaction specification (e.g., bulk delete, filter)

evals.prompt: Set up a design system for a SaaS dashboard app. Create: design tokens (colors with primary/gray/semantic, spacing scale, typography), a Tailwind config that extends from the tokens, a Button component with variants (primary/secondary/ghost/danger) and sizes (sm/md/lg) using CVA, and a dark mode theme provider.
evals.expected_output: Token files, Tailwind config extending tokens, Button component with CVA variants and forwardRef, ThemeProvider with dark mode toggle, proper TypeScript types

assertions.name: token_files
assertions.description: Separate token definitions for colors, spacing, and typography as TypeScript constants

assertions.name: tailwind_extends_tokens
assertions.description: Tailwind config imports and extends from token files, not hardcoded values

assertions.name: cva_button
assertions.description: Button uses class-variance-authority (CVA) with variant and size props

assertions.name: forward_ref
assertions.description: Button uses forwardRef for DOM access

assertions.name: dark_mode
assertions.description: ThemeProvider or dark mode implementation with class toggle or CSS variables

assertions.name: accessibility
assertions.description: Button has focus-visible styles and aria attributes for loading/disabled states

evals.prompt: I'm about to make my private repo public on GitHub. It's a Node.js/Express app that has been in development for 6 months. Scan for any secrets that might be committed and give me a remediation plan.
evals.expected_output: Gitleaks scan command, vendor pattern list to check, file types to flag, remediation steps (rotate, remove from history, gitignore, pre-commit hook), prevention setup

assertions.name: scan_command
assertions.description: Provides gitleaks detect command or equivalent scanning approach

assertions.name: vendor_patterns
assertions.description: Lists specific vendor patterns to check (AWS keys, GitHub tokens, Stripe keys, etc.)

assertions.name: file_flags
assertions.description: Flags .env files, .pem/.key files, and other sensitive file types

assertions.name: rotate_first
assertions.description: Emphasizes rotating/revoking secrets BEFORE removing from git history

assertions.name: history_cleanup
assertions.description: Includes git history cleanup (BFG or git-filter-repo)

assertions.name: prevention_setup
assertions.description: Sets up pre-commit hook and .gitignore to prevent future leaks

evals.prompt: Run a dependency audit on our NestJS backend and React frontend monorepo. We use npm workspaces. Check for vulnerabilities, outdated packages, and license issues. Prioritize findings and give me a remediation plan.
evals.expected_output: npm audit command, triage by severity+reachability, license check, supply chain checks, structured output table, remediation strategies

assertions.name: audit_command
assertions.description: Provides npm audit or equivalent vulnerability scanning command

assertions.name: severity_triage
assertions.description: Triages findings by severity AND reachability, not just CVSS score alone

assertions.name: license_check
assertions.description: Checks license compatibility (flags GPL/AGPL in proprietary code)

assertions.name: supply_chain
assertions.description: Checks for lockfile existence, unpinned deps, suspicious scripts

assertions.name: structured_output
assertions.description: Produces a structured findings table with package, CVE, severity, fix version

assertions.name: remediation_plan
assertions.description: Provides specific remediation strategies (upgrade, override, replace)

evals.prompt: Estimate the effort to add a real-time notification system to our NestJS + React app. It needs: WebSocket connection with Socket.IO, notification types (info, warning, error), a notification center UI with read/unread state, push notification support for mobile (via Firebase), and a notification preferences page.
evals.expected_output: Feature decomposed into tasks, each scored on 5 complexity factors, risk multipliers applied, estimate as a range with confidence level

assertions.name: task_decomposition
assertions.description: Feature broken into 5+ concrete tasks before estimating

assertions.name: complexity_scoring
assertions.description: Each task scored on complexity factors (code changes, new concepts, dependencies, testing, risk)

assertions.name: size_mapping
assertions.description: Total score mapped to S/M/L/XL with story points

assertions.name: risk_multipliers
assertions.description: Risk multipliers applied (e.g., new technology for WebSocket/Firebase)

assertions.name: range_estimate
assertions.description: Final estimate given as a range, not a single number

assertions.name: confidence_level
assertions.description: Confidence level stated (Low/Medium/High) with reasoning

evals.prompt: I want to add a multi-tenant billing system to our SaaS app. It needs subscription plans, usage-based billing, Stripe integration, invoice generation, and an admin billing dashboard. Set up the spec-driven development workflow for this feature.
evals.expected_output: Spec directory created, requirements.md template with user stories, design.md template with API/schema, tasks.md with ordered implementation tasks, phase gates defined

assertions.name: spec_directory
assertions.description: Creates .claude/specs/billing/ or similar spec directory structure

assertions.name: requirements_template
assertions.description: requirements.md with user stories, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries (in/out)

assertions.name: design_template
assertions.description: design.md covering API endpoints, data model, and error handling

assertions.name: tasks_template
assertions.description: tasks.md with ordered tasks, dependencies, verification commands

assertions.name: phase_gates
assertions.description: Phase gates defined (approval required before advancing)

assertions.name: enforcement_rules
assertions.description: Enforcement rules stated (no code before design, no implementation before tasks)

evals.prompt: Scaffold a new NestJS + PostgreSQL backend project for a task management API. Set up the project structure, Prisma ORM, JWT authentication, environment configuration, and initial tests.
evals.expected_output: NestJS project structure with modules, Prisma schema, auth module with JWT, .env.example, initial test, package.json with dependencies

assertions.name: nestjs_structure
assertions.description: Creates NestJS project structure with src/, modules, controllers, services

assertions.name: prisma_setup
assertions.description: Includes Prisma schema file with initial model

assertions.name: auth_template
assertions.description: Auth module with JWT register/login/refresh flow

assertions.name: env_example
assertions.description: .env.example with all required environment variables (placeholders only)

assertions.name: initial_test
assertions.description: At least one test file that verifies the app starts

assertions.name: scope_boundary
assertions.description: Does NOT include React/frontend setup (stays within backend scope)

evals.prompt: Design a state machine for an e-commerce order lifecycle. States should include: draft, placed, payment_pending, payment_failed, confirmed, preparing, shipped, delivered, return_requested, returned, cancelled. Include guard conditions, side effects, a Mermaid diagram, and TypeScript implementation.
evals.expected_output: State table with descriptions and terminal flags, transition table with guards and side effects, Mermaid stateDiagram, TypeScript implementation with const enum + transition map, validation checklist

assertions.name: state_table
assertions.description: All 11 states listed in a table with descriptions and terminal flags

assertions.name: transition_table
assertions.description: Transition table with From, To, Trigger, Guard, and Side Effects columns

assertions.name: guard_conditions
assertions.description: Guard conditions defined (e.g., payment verified, items in stock)

assertions.name: side_effects
assertions.description: Side effects listed (e.g., send email, update inventory, create audit log)

assertions.name: mermaid_diagram
assertions.description: Mermaid stateDiagram-v2 showing all states and transitions

assertions.name: typescript_impl
assertions.description: TypeScript implementation using const enum + transition map pattern (not switch statements)
